name: Papaya Liqueur
recipeInstructions:
1. Select a medium-sized papaya (slightly larger than a pear) with smooth, unbruised skin and a fruity aroma. The best time is May and June.
2. Avoid dark spots on the skin as they are a bad sign and will cause an unpleasant flavor. Check the stem for decay or softness.
3. Cut the papaya in half and remove the seeds. Peel off the skin.
4. Cut the papaya into medium chunks.
5. Place the papaya pieces in a glass jar with the vodka and lemon peel (twist to release its flavors).
6. Allow the mixture to steep for one week, stirring occasionally.
7. Strain the mixture through a fine mesh strainer or cheesecloth into another clean glass jar.
8. Squeeze out any remaining juice from the papaya pieces.
9. If desired, add sugar syrup (optional). Adjust to taste by adding more or less sugar.
10. Store the liqueur in a sealed container for up to three weeks before consuming.
